name: thalamic_relay
description: Thalamic relay — sensory gating circuit. The thalamus is the brain's switchboard: ALL sensory input passes through it before reaching cortex. Gate open = signal passes. Gate closed = signal blocked. Mechanism: 2 RS relay neurons receive sensory input. 2 FS gate neurons receive cortical feedback (attention signal). When gate FS fires, it SUPPRESSES relay, blocking signal. When gate is silent (attention present), relay passes signal through. LTS neuron provides rebound burst after gate releases — this is how the thalamus amplifies signals you've been waiting for. Implements: selective attention, sensory filtering, sleep gating.
